title: Workers
description: Choose and configure you-agent-factory workers: runtime executors declared in the factory and invoked by workstations, with six Factory WorkerType variants plus a separate mock-worker surface.
openingSummary: A worker is a named runtime executor a workstation invokes by name. factory.json declares each worker; the WorkerType discriminator selects the implementation family. Pick the matching variant page before you author type-specific fields, and keep mock workers on their own test-only schema.

howToUse.title: How To Use
howToUse.body: Use the selection table to open the WorkerType page that matches the work. Prefer current public WorkerType names in new configs; legacy MODEL_WORKER and HOSTED_WORKER remain loadable aliases with their own migration pages. Author shared identity fields such as name, type, body, timeout, and resources before variant-specific configuration. Mock workers are not a Factory WorkerType — keep them on the mock-workers schema for deterministic test dispatch only.

selection.title: Select A Worker Variant
selection.body: Use the comparison below to open the matching variant page. Prefer current public WorkerType names in new configs. Legacy MODEL_WORKER and HOSTED_WORKER remain loadable aliases and have their own pages for migration review.

sharedFields.title: Shared Fields
sharedFields.body: These fields apply across Factory Worker variants. Variant pages add type-specific fields such as model routing, command and args, or hosted provider auth. The live Worker schema embed below shows the base contract from the installed Factory package.

workerSchema.title: Worker Schema
workerSchema.body: The embed shows the live Factory Worker base definition from the installed package. Open the full Factory schema reference for exhaustive field lookup rather than copying contract text into this index.

limitsAndAssumptions.title: Limits And Assumptions
limitsAndAssumptions.body: This index is a Worker family overview and selection surface. It is not a sync of packaged CLI docs, not a Workstation routing guide, and not a full per-variant field dump. Variant pages own discriminator-specific configuration. Mock workers stay on their own page and schema. Workstation companion pages are discovery links only.

links.selectionInferenceWhen: One-shot harness inference such as TTS, ASR, or typed INFERENCE_RUN dispatches
links.selectionAgentWhen: Agent-loop execution for AGENT_RUN workstations until acceptance, rejection, or failure
links.selectionPollerWhen: Hosted poller ingress paired with workstation behavior POLLER
links.selectionModelWhen: Legacy managed or local model capability alias — prefer INFERENCE_WORKER or AGENT_WORKER for new configs
links.selectionHostedWhen: Legacy hosted provider alias — prefer POLLER_WORKER for new configs
links.selectionMockWhen: Test-only deterministic accept, script, or reject dispatch from the mock-workers schema
links.selectionMockNote: Mock workers are not a seventh Factory WorkerType. Keep them on the mock-workers schema and the mock worker page.
links.sharedNameRole: Worker identity declared in the factory and bound by workstations
links.sharedTypeRole: WorkerType discriminator that selects the implementation family
links.sharedBodyRole: Shared markdown body — system prompt for agent workers; descriptive for other types
links.sharedTimeoutRole: Execution time bound for the worker run
links.sharedResourcesRole: Resource claims the worker needs at runtime
